questions.question: What are the four dimensions tracked in the Night and Day Reflection Log?

options.text: Hours slept, caffeine intake, exercise, and screen time
Sleep quality, mood and emotions, focus and clarity, and connections noticed
Bedtime, wake time, naps, and dreams
Stress level, appetite, energy, and pain

questions.explanation: The reflection log tracks four dimensions each day: sleep quality (rate 1-10 or describe), mood and emotions, focus and clarity, and connections noticed between sleep and your day.

questions.question: How often should you review your reflection log entries to notice patterns?

options.text: Only at the end of 30 days

questions.explanation: After a week or two, reviewing your entries allows you to start noticing patterns between your sleep quality and your mood, focus, or energy. Weekly review is recommended.

questions.question: What does the lesson emphasize matters more than writing detailed entries?

options.text: Using a digital app to track sleep
Consistency in filling out the log
Writing at least a full page each day

questions.explanation: Consistency matters more than detail. A quick entry like '7/10, calm, focused, slept well' is perfectly useful. The key is building a gentle habit of noticing over time.

questions.question: What is described as the 'most valuable data you have' for sleep improvement?

options.text: Your own personal experience and observations

questions.explanation: Your own experience is the most valuable data you have. The reflection log helps you discover personal patterns that are uniquely yours -- patterns that no textbook could ever teach you.

questions.question: Who is suggested as someone to share your reflection log with?
questions.explanation: If you are working with a healthcare provider, the reflection log can be an incredibly useful tool to bring to appointments, helping them understand your sleep patterns.

title: The Night and Day Reflection Log - Quiz
